segments.caption: The video captures the intense final stretch of the 100 metres decathlon event at the World Athletics Championships Oregon 22. The race begins with athletes sprinting down the red track, with Warner (Canada, lane 3) leading the pack, followed closely by Lepage (Canada, lane 1) and Moloney (Australia, lane 6). The camera follows the runners as they approach the finish line, where Warner crosses first with an unofficial time of 10.28 seconds under a +0.8 m/s wind. After the race, the athletes slow down and begin to walk off the track, displaying a mix of exhaustion and satisfaction. Warner, visibly pleased, turns to acknowledge the crowd and his competitors. Lepage, in his red and white striped singlet, walks with a composed demeanor, while Moloney, in green and yellow, bends over to catch his breath. Other athletes, including Ziemek (USA, lane 4), Victor (Grenada, lane 5), and Owens-Delermé (Puerto Rico, lane 8), are seen interacting and recovering. The stadium is filled with spectators, and banners for 'Oregon 22' and 'World Athletics Championships' are visible. The on-screen graphics display the unofficial results, with Warner in first, Lepage second, and Moloney third. The atmosphere is charged with post-race energy as athletes regroup and prepare for the next event.

segments.caption: The video captures the intense final moments of the 400 metres decathlon event at the World Athletics Championships Oregon 22. As the race ends, Õiglane collapses onto the track, lying on his back in exhaustion, clutching his chest and gasping for air, clearly spent from the effort. He slowly sits up, still breathing heavily, while the unofficial results graphic confirms his victory with a time of 49.18 seconds, ahead of Mullings (+0.14), Ziemek (+0.46), and Uibo (+1.23). The camera follows the athletes' post-race reactions, capturing their physical and emotional states after the grueling race.
